title: draba pilosa var. polytricha
intro: Draba pilosa var. polytricha, also known as the hairy draba, is a variety of flowering plant in the mustard family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Draba pilosa var. polytricha include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, or topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a treatment.
